title: Payton Tolle gets standing ovation after tossing seven scoreless innings vs. Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle threw seven scoreless innings against the Yankees, allowing one hit and striking out seven.
Tolle carried a perfect game bid into the sixth inning before losing it but still received a standing ovation.
Despite battling an illness, Tolle helped Boston go up 2-0 in the four-game series against the Yankees at Fenway Park.

title: 5 ways Celtics could improve lineup without trading Jaylen Brown
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Celtics could trade Derrick White and Baylor Scheierman for Trey Murphy and Yves Missi to improve the roster.
The team may seek a center upgrade through trade or free agency, considering players like Mitchell Robinson and Zach Collins.
Boston might offload Sam Hauser's contract for more salary flexibility, potentially using rookies to fill his role.

title: 5 things to know about new Bruins forward JJ Peterka
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: JJ Peterka was acquired by the Boston Bruins from the Utah Mammoth in exchange for two first-round draft picks.
Peterka is a speedy German winger with a scoring average of 26.6 goals over the last three NHL seasons.
Peterka and his family built a hockey rink in Buchbach, Germany, aimed at promoting hockey among young players in Bavaria.

title: NBA Rumor Roundup: The latest reports on the Jaylen Brown trade offers
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics are reportedly considering trade offers for Jaylen Brown from teams like Detroit Pistons and Portland Trail Blazers.
Celtics demand at least four first-round picks for Brown; various player and pick combinations remain possible.
Portland, with draft capital and former Celtics players, and Detroit, considering a sign-and-trade, have shown interest.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' and 'Superman' are among new movies available on Disney+ and HBO Max.
'Avatar The Last Airbender' Season 2 and 'The Bear' Season 5 stream on Netflix and Hulu.
'Life, Larry and the Pursuit of Unhappiness' features Larry David's historical comedy on HBO Max.

title: Red Sox place Marcelo Mayer on 10-day IL with forearm issue
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Marcelo Mayer placed on 10-day injured list by Boston Red Sox due to a forearm issue.
Tsung-Che Cheng to debut at shortstop for Red Sox against Yankees, replacing Mayer.
Red Sox struggle with injuries, affecting prospects Mayer, Roman Anthony, and player Trevor Story.

title: Bruins reportedly trade for Utah forward JJ Peterka
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins are finalizing a trade for Utah Mammoth's JJ Peterka, sending two first-round picks in exchange.
Peterka, 24, has recorded 20-plus goals in each of the last three NHL seasons.
Peterka's contract runs through the 2029-30 season, carrying a $7.7 million cap hit per year.

title: Craig Breslow on his job security, whether the Red Sox will buy or sell, and more
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Craig Breslow, Red Sox chief baseball officer, remains optimistic despite team's last-place standing and recent losses.
Breslow acknowledges possibility of selling at trade deadline but still believes in current roster's potential.
Breslow focuses on internal improvements and avoids discussing his job security directly with owner John Henry.

title: Review: ‘Supergirl’ is a hero in search of a better movie
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Milly Alcock stars in 'Supergirl,' but the film is described as bland and derivative by critics.
Director Craig Gillespie attempts to inject comedy through the interaction between Kara and the character Ruthye.
'Supergirl' features unoriginal CGI and uninspired character designs, drawing comparisons to 'Mad Max.'

title: One of Cambridge’s most beloved Japanese restaurants is closing after 34 years
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Tampopo in Cambridge is closing after 34 years as owner Yasumasa Ito retires.
Tampopo, located at 1815 Massachusetts Ave., will serve its last meal on June 30.
Former employee Kotaro Morita returned from Japan to assist during Tampopo's final weeks.
